title: Experience Compression Spectrum: Unifying Memory, Skills, and Rules in LLM Agents
abstract: arXiv:2604.15877v2 Announce Type: replace  Abstract: As LLM agents scale to long-horizon, multi-session deployments, efficiently managing accumulated experience becomes a critical bottleneck. Agent memory systems and agent skill discovery both address this challenge, extracting reusable knowledge from interaction traces, yet a citation analysis of 1{,}136 references across 22 primary papers reveals a cross-community citation rate below 1\%. We propose the \emph{Experience Compression Spectrum}, a unifying framework that positions memory, skills, and rules as points along a single axis of increasing compression (5--20$\times$ for episodic memory, 50--500$\times$ for procedural skills, 1{,}000$\times$+ for declarative rules), directly reducing context consumption, retrieval latency, and compute overhead. Mapping 20+ systems onto this spectrum reveals that every system operates at a fixed, predetermined compression level: none supports adaptive cross-level compression, a gap we term the \

Title: Experience Compression Spectrum: Unifying Memory, Skills, and Rules in LLM Agents
Abstract: arXiv:2604.15877v2 Announce Type: replace  Abstract: As LLM agents scale to long-horizon, multi-session deployments, efficiently managing accumulated experience becomes a critical bottleneck. Agent memory systems and agent skill discovery both address this challenge, extracting reusable knowledge from interaction traces, yet a citation analysis of 1{,}136 references across 22 primary papers reveals a cross-community citation rate below 1\%. We propose the \emph{Experience Compression Spectrum}, a unifying framework that positions memory, skills, and rules as points along a single axis of increasing compression (5--20$\times$ for episodic memory, 50--500$\times$ for procedural skills, 1{,}000$\times$+ for declarative rules), directly reducing context consumption, retrieval latency, and compute overhead. Mapping 20+ systems onto this spectrum reveals that every system operates at a fixed, predetermined compression level: none supports adaptive cross-level compression, a gap we term the \
translated_title: 经验压缩谱：统一大语言模型智能体中的记忆、技能与规则
translated_abstract: arXiv:2604.15877v2 公告类型：替换 摘要：随着大语言模型智能体扩展到长周期、多会话部署场景，有效管理累积经验成为关键瓶颈。智能体记忆系统和智能体技能发现都致力于解决这一挑战，从交互轨迹中提取可重用知识，然而对22篇主要论文中1,136篇参考文献的引文分析显示，跨社区的引用率低于1%。我们提出“经验压缩谱”这一统一框架，将记忆、技能和规则定位在一条递增压缩率的单一轴上（情景记忆压缩5-20倍，程序性技能压缩50-500倍，陈述性规则压缩1000倍以上），直接降低上下文消耗、检索延迟和计算开销。将20多个系统映射到这个谱系上揭示：每个系统都运行在固定的、预定的压缩级别上：没有系统支持自适应的跨级别压缩，我们将这一空白称为“压缩鸿沟”。
tldr: 本文提出“经验压缩谱”框架，将记忆、技能和规则统一为不同压缩程度的连续谱，并揭示现有系统缺乏自适应跨级别压缩能力这一关键空白。
en_tdlr: This paper proposes the "Experience Compression Spectrum" framework, unifying memory, skills, and rules along a continuum of varying compression levels, and reveals a critical gap where existing systems lack adaptive cross-level compression.
